Sun Direct Renewable · Alberta

Solar Incentives and Financing in Alberta

Explore current solar financing, micro-generation credits, municipal programs and business tax incentives available to qualifying Alberta properties.

Get Your Solar AssessmentExplore Current Options

Last reviewed: July 22, 2026

Home/Solar Incentives and Financing in Alberta
On this page
Calgary CEIPMicro-generationBusiness taxWays to payFAQ

Start with the right category

Current options deserve a careful review.

Programs, credits and financing are different tools. Start with your property, applicant type and electricity use—then confirm the official rules that apply.

Calgary property-based financing

A municipal financing path for eligible Calgary properties, repaid through property taxes.

Explore

Alberta micro-generation credits

Understand on-site use, exported electricity and retailer billing before a system is designed.

Explore

Business tax incentives

A starting point for organizations reviewing qualifying clean-technology property with tax advice.

Explore

Solar financing options

Compare ownership and repayment paths around your property, project and lender requirements.

Explore

Alberta does not currently offer a universal provincial residential solar rebate. Available financing, tax incentives and municipal programs depend on the applicant, property, municipality and project.

Calgary residential CEIP

Property-based financing for eligible Calgary homes.

Calgary’s Residential Clean Energy Improvement Program is financing—not a rebate. It can help approved homeowners fund eligible clean-energy improvements through their property tax bill.

  • May finance up to 100% of eligible project costs, to $50,000
  • Repayment is made through the property tax bill and can extend up to 20 years
  • Financing remains attached to the property, subject to the agreement
  • Approval and program agreements are required before work starts
View Official Program Information

Review CEIP requirements directly

Property eligibility, financing terms and application requirements are set and confirmed by the official program administrator. Sun Direct can provide solar project information when requested, but does not determine program eligibility.

Alberta Municipalities CEIP

Calgary commercial CEIP

Commercial financing needs a property-by-property review.

Eligible Calgary commercial properties may be able to finance renewable-energy improvements through CEIP. It is a program for approved commercial retrofits, not a universal business incentive.

Intake currently closed

Review official pre-qualification information and program updates before planning an application.

  • May finance up to 100% of eligible costs, to $1 million
  • Repayment is collected through the property tax bill
  • Projects must not begin before approval and agreements
  • New builds, farmland and industrial-tax-class properties may not qualify
Request a Commercial Solar AssessmentView Commercial CEIP

Alberta micro-generation credits

Use power first. Export the excess.

Micro-generation is small-scale electricity production designed around a property’s own electricity needs—not a commercial power-generation project.

Solar Production
On-Site Use
Excess Export
Retailer Credit

What a solar system can do

Electricity used directly on-site can reduce grid consumption. Excess electricity supplied to the grid may receive retailer credits under the retailer’s terms.

What still needs review

Retailer rates and contracts vary. Distribution, transmission, local access and other fixed charges may remain, even when a system produces power.

What must be approved

Utility interconnection and micro-generation requirements apply. The wire service provider reviews the notice to become a micro-generator.

Get a Usage-Based Solar ProposalRead Official Micro-Generation Guidance

Federal business tax incentives

Tax treatment should be confirmed before it is assumed.

The federal Clean Technology Investment Tax Credit may provide a refundable tax credit for qualifying new clean-technology property. Eligible solar or storage property can depend on ownership, tax status, acquisition date and use.

It is not presented here as a residential homeowner incentive, and Sun Direct Renewable does not calculate or promise a credit amount.

Sun Direct Renewable does not provide tax advice. Confirm eligibility and claim requirements with a qualified Canadian tax professional.

Discuss a Commercial Solar ProjectRead CRA Guidance

Questions for a tax professional

  • Who owns the qualifying property?
  • What is the entity’s Canadian tax status?
  • When is the property acquired and available for use?
  • Does the equipment and intended use meet the current rules?

Farm and agricultural solar

Farm solar starts with the way the operation uses power.

Barns, workshops, grain operations, irrigation and cold storage each create a different load profile. Rooftop and ground-mounted opportunities need to be reviewed against service capacity, land classification, municipal requirements and working access.

  • Micro-generation and export-focused generation have different planning paths
  • Larger land-based projects can require agricultural and municipal impact review
  • Business tax incentives may be relevant where an eligible taxable entity operates the farm
  • No grant, financing or tax treatment should be assumed without an individual review
Request a Farm Solar Assessment

Ways to pay for solar

Compare the path, not just the monthly number.

Every option should be reviewed around ownership, total cost, program availability and your property’s project scope.

Cash purchase

Full ownership with no financing interest. Pricing is shaped around the actual system design.

Solar financing

Spread cost over time. Approval, rates and total borrowing cost depend on the lender and agreement.

Property-based financing

Available only through participating municipal programs, with property-tax repayment and intake limits.

Commercial project financing

Structured around project size, ownership and business requirements, with individual review required.

Incentive eligibility checklist

What should be reviewed before you rely on an option.

Program fit is a set of connected checks—not a single yes or no. We can help organize project information, then point you to the appropriate program, utility, lender or tax professional.

Check My Property
  • Municipality
  • Property classification
  • Ownership
  • Applicant or business tax status
  • Electricity consumption
  • Utility service area
  • Roof or land suitability
  • Project start date
  • Program intake status
  • Required approvals
  • Tax and lender eligibility

Our assessment process

A clear way to move from questions to a project plan.

Sun Direct Renewable can help organize project information, but cannot guarantee approval, funding or tax eligibility.

  1. 01

    Review Your Electricity Usage

  2. 02

    Confirm Property and Program Eligibility

  3. 03

    Prepare a Custom Solar and Savings Proposal

  4. 04

    Explain Financing and Application Next Steps

Frequently asked questions

Current answers for Alberta solar planning.

Is there currently an Alberta residential solar rebate?+

Alberta does not currently offer a universal provincial residential solar rebate. Municipal programs, financing and other opportunities can depend on the applicant, property, municipality and current intake.

Is Calgary CEIP a rebate or a loan?+

CEIP is property-based financing, not a rebate. Approved financing is repaid through the property tax bill under the program agreement.

Can I begin installation before CEIP approval?+

No. Calgary states that the application must be approved and the Clean Energy Improvement Agreement entered before work begins for CEIP financing.

Will solar remove every charge from my electricity bill?+

No. Electricity used on-site can reduce grid consumption, but distribution, transmission, local access and other charges can remain.

How are exported solar credits calculated?+

For micro-generation, excess electricity supplied to the grid is credited by the retailer. Retailer rates, terms and billing arrangements should be confirmed directly with that retailer.

Can farms qualify for solar incentives?+

Farm eligibility depends on the property, business structure, utility arrangement, land classification and current program rules. Farms should not assume a grant or financing program applies automatically.

Can a business claim the Clean Technology ITC?+

Qualifying new clean-technology property may be eligible for a refundable federal credit, subject to detailed ownership, tax-status, acquisition and use requirements. A Canadian tax professional should confirm eligibility.

Can I combine financing with a tax incentive?+

It may be possible in some situations, but financing terms and tax eligibility are separate decisions. Confirm lender, program and tax treatment before relying on any combination.

What happens to CEIP financing if the property is sold?+

CEIP financing is attached to the property. The owner may be able to repay the balance or disclose and transfer it to a new owner, subject to the program agreement.

How often should incentive information be reviewed?+

Review it before design, before signing a contract and again before an application. Intake, eligibility, retailer terms and tax rules can change.

Your next step

Share your electricity usage, property type and location. We’ll review your solar potential and help identify the financing or incentive categories worth exploring.
